title: Lines Written In "Letters Of An Italian Nun And An  English Gentleman, By J. J. Rousseau; [1]  Founded On Facts."
content:
"Away, away, - your flattering arts
May now betray some simpler hearts;
And you will smile at their believing,
And they shall weep at your deceiving."
meaning:
George Gordon Byron's contribution to classical-poetry is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Lines Written In "Letters Of An Italian Nun And An  English Gentleman, By J. J. Rousseau; [1]  Founded On Facts.""...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
